finding: §10.9 couples the absolute safety claim 'tp never silently matches a positional id against a slug id' to the tp audit --merge discussion (the immediately preceding sentences describe merge dedup on (role, item_id)), but the ONLY safeguard it names — 'a prior-round section built from one states its ids are positional and not comparable' — is the prompt-emission path (§10.2), not the merge path. For --merge itself the spec states only 'deduplicates its input rows on (role, item_id)' with no id_scheme marker check, so a tester cannot write a deterministic pass/fail test for cross-scheme merge: if merge dedups purely on (role, item_id), a coincidentally-equal positional/slug pair (e.g. file-go-safety-2 from a legacy ordinal vs a slug whose subject yields '2') is silently collapsed — contradicting the 'never silently matches' claim; if merge reads the id_scheme marker to skip cross-scheme pairs, the spec never says so. No acceptance test covers cross-scheme merge (#12 is two slug-scheme rounds; #18 asserts a legacy round is READ and produces a prior-round section, not that --merge handles mixed inputs). Two engineers would write contradictory tests for the same claim.
suggestion: Pin what tp audit --merge does with mixed-scheme inputs: state explicitly that merge reads each input round's id_scheme marker and deduplicates only within the same scheme (a positional id is never collapsed against a slug id even if textually equal), or that a marker-less input is treated as positional and its rows are exempt from cross-round dedup. Then add an acceptance test feeding one legacy (no marker) + one slug round to --merge and asserting the cross-scheme pair is NOT deduped.

finding: §10.2's prior-round section emits a 'file changed since' flag keyed on each non-PASS row's evidence_file, but per the audit NDJSON schema (0.23.0 §4.2) evidence_file is null for FAIL rows. FAIL is the primary defect type, so the changed-since signal — the very mechanism §10.2 introduces so an auditor can distinguish a first-seen defect from one a later commit already fixed — is structurally absent for the rows where it matters most. The spec says the flag is 'omitted when the row has no file path (e.g. spec-derived items)' but never connects this to the FAIL case, so an implementer building it finds the signal only ever fires on PARTIAL rows and §10.2's stated purpose is only half-met.
suggestion: State explicitly in §10.2 that changed-since covers only file-bearing rows (PARTIAL) and that prior FAIL defects carry no machine signal and must be re-checked by hand; or, in a later release, capture the defect's file path on FAIL rows so the signal reaches them too.

finding: The audit prior-round section lists each of the role's non-PASS rows with a `role` field that the spec itself admits is redundant with the section's role scope (every row is the same role), retained only for human debugging readability. It feeds no agent decision — the auditor already knows its own role from the section it was handed — yet it recurs in every non-PASS row of every audit prompt from round 2 onward, the exact byte-the-agent-parses-but-never-acts-on case. Not blocking: a deliberate, documented tradeoff, below the convergence bar.

The cross-round rewrite reverted round-6's intra-round guarantee. The claim 'tp never silently matches a positional id against a slug id' is backed only for the prior-round section (reads the round record with the marker), not the merge path (§10.4 globs marker-less audit-r<N>-<role>.ndjson files). §10.9 doesn't state how merge detects/refuses a legacy result file. Practical impact ~0 (slugs always carry letters, positional ids are bare digits, so they cannot collide), but the claim overreaches merge's data source.
